title: System and method for curved light sheet projection during two-photon polymerization
abstract: A laser-based manufacturing system is disclosed for fabricating non-planar three-dimensional layers. The system may have a laser for producing a laser beam with a plurality of optical wavelengths. An optically dispersive element may be used for receiving the laser beam and splitting the beam into a plurality of distinct beam components, wherein each beam component has spatially separated optical spectral components. A phase mask may be used which is configured to receive at least one of the beam components emerging from the dispersive element and to create a modified beam. One or more focusing elements may then be used to receive the modified beam emerging from the phase mask and to focus the modified beam into a non-planar light sheet for use in fabricating a part.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A laser-based manufacturing system for fabricating non-planar three- dimensional layers, the system comprising: a laser for producing a laser beam with a plurality of optical wavelengths; an optically dispersive element for receiving the laser beam and splitting the beam into a plurality of distinct beam components, wherein each said beam component has spatially separated optical spectral components; a phase mask configured to receive at least one of the beam components emerging from the dispersive element; and one or more focusing elements configured to receive a modified beam emerging from the phase mask and to focus the modified beam into a non-planar light sheet. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein the one or more focusing elements includes a mirror. 3. The system of claim 1 , wherein the claim 2, wherein the mirror comprises a curved mirror. 4. The system of claim 1 , wherein the one or more focusing elements includes an objective lens for receiving the modified beam from the phase mask. 5. The system of claim 1 , wherein the one or more focusing elements includes: a mirror for receiving the modified beam from the phase mask; and an objective lens for receiving the modified beam after the beam has been reflected by the mirror. 6. The system of claim 1 , wherein the dispersive element comprises a fixed grating or a flexible grating. 7. The system of claim 1 , wherein the dispersive element comprises a digital micro-mirror device (DMD) or a spatial light modulator (SLM). 8. The system of claim 1 , wherein the dispersive element patterns each of said beam components into regions of high and low intensity. 9. The system of claim 1 , wherein the phase mask comprises a phase plate. 10. The system of claim 1 , wherein the phase mask comprises at least one of a digital micro-mirror device (DMD) or a spatial light modulator (SLM). 11. The system of claim 1 , wherein the phase mask comprises a computer generated hologram (CGH) module. 12. The system of claiml , further comprising an electronic controller for controlling at least one of: the laser, the dispersive element and the phase mask. 13. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a movable stage for supporting a material to be acted upon by the non-planar light sheet. 14. The system of claim 1 , further comprising: a lamp for generating a light beam for illuminating a material being acted on by the non-planar light sheet; a camera; and a beam splitter for directing the light beam from the lamp toward the camera, wherein an output from the camera is used to image the material, in situ, being operated on by the non-planar light sheet. 15. The system of claim 1 further comprising a beam expander configured to expand the laser beam being output from the laser. 16. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a beam homogenizer for acting on the laser beam to improve an intensity uniformity of the laser beam. 17. A laser-based manufacturing system for fabricating non-planar three- dimensional layers, the system comprising: a laser for producing a laser beam with a plurality of optical wavelengths; an optically dispersive element for receiving the laser beam and splitting the laser beam into a plurality of distinct beam components, wherein each said beam component has spatially separated optical spectral components; a digitally tunable phase mask configured to receive at least one of the beam components emerging from the dispersive element; a controller for controlling the phase mask; and a focusing subsystem including a mirror and an objective lens configured to receive a modified beam emerging from the phase mask and to focus the modified beam into a non-planar light sheet. 18. The system of claim 17, further comprising a camera for imaging an operation performed using the non-planar light sheet on a material. 19. The system of claim 17, wherein the phase mask comprises one of: a phase plate; a digital micro-mirror device (DMD); a spatial light modulator (SLM); or a computer generated hologram module. 20. The system of claim 17, wherein the dispersive element comprises one of: a fixed grating; a flexible grating; a digital micro-mirror device; or a spatial light modulator (SLM). 21. A method for fabricating non-planar three-dimensional layers using a photo responsive material, the method comprising: generating a laser beam having a plurality of optical wavelengths; using an optically dispersive element to receive the laser beam and splitting the laser beam into a plurality of distinct beam components, wherein each said beam component has spatially separated optical spectral components; receiving at least one of the beam components emerging from the dispersive element with a phase mask and using the phase mask to produce a modified beam having a phase pattern; and using one or more focusing elements configured to receive the modified beam emerging from the phase mask and to focus the modified beam into a non-planar light sheet.
